pages.raw_text:
r. Fl. Cosgrove, who has been con-
nected with the management of fairs
in Minnesota all his life, arrived in
Spokane last evening and was met
by Howell w. Peel, president, and
II. G. Stimmel, manager of the Spo-
kane Interstate Fair. Mr.. Cosgrove
was shown around the city this morn
ing and then taken to the fair grounds
where he examined the track and the
grounds. He was much pleased with
the grounds and the race track in
particular, and said that the track was
one of the finest for half-mile track
that he had seen in this country.


''rhe racing outlook for next year
is splendid said he Montana will
have one of the largest meets in the
west next fall, and there is no reason
why the management of the Spokane
Fair should not obtain some of the
best racers in the circuit. Spokane is
well fitted, from what | have observed
for splendid fair next year. l have
been told of the magnificent wheat
crops and the large farms in eastern
Washington and am surprised at the
growth of the state and Spokane, in
particular, in the last three years. !
was in Spokane three years ago, and
now, on my return, am surprised.


From one of the cattlemen | have
heard of the blooded stock in this
vicinity and especially about the Short
horn stock Taking the horses, cattle
aid farming products as exhibits the
fair next year should be a record
breaker"


Mr. Cosgrove will meet with the
management Of the fair this afternoon
at o'clock iii the Office of Superin-
tendent Huntington. in the Empire
State building, at which time plans for
the coming fair will be discussed.


There is no doubt in the minds Of
those who have met Mr.. Cosgrove that
he will be the next manager of the
Interstate Fair. Mr.. Stimmel, Mr..
Peel and Mr.. Huntington are all in
favor of engaging his services.
